content.text:
[Workflow Task from Orchestrator]
Workflow ID: fd751354-d56
Subtask ID: f6c2483d
Priority: 1

Task: Create openclaw.json config for eta-manager (Legal & Compliance Manager). Port 18818. Uses Kimi K2.5. Role: Lead legal compliance, bar regulations, data privacy, regulatory requirements for legal tech products. Include proper NVIDIA API config.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to create an openclaw.json config file for a new agent called "eta-manager" which is the Legal & Compliance Manager. Let me gather the requirements:

1. Agent name: eta-manager
2. Port: 18818
3. Model: Kimi K2.5 (nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5)
4. Role: Legal & Compliance Manager
5. Responsibilities: Lead legal compliance, bar regulations, data privacy, regulatory requirements for legal tech products
6. Include proper NVIDIA API config

I should look at existing agent configs to understand the structure. Let me check what other managers have in their configs.

Looking at the context, I can see this is part of the ai_final hierarchy with:
- King AI at port 18789
- Alpha Manager (me) at port 18800, managing general-1 through general-4 (18811-18814)
- Beta Manager (coding) at port 18801
- Gamma Manager (research) at port 18802

So eta-manager at 18818 would be the Legal & Compliance Manager - a specialized manager role.

I need to create a proper openclaw.json configuration. Let me first check if there's an existing config structure I can reference

content.text: I'll create the openclaw.json config for eta-manager (Legal & Compliance Manager). Let me first check the

content.thinking: Let me check an existing manager config to get the structure right, particularly looking at alpha-manager or beta-manager

Good, I have the structure. Now I need to create the eta-manager config. Key differences for a manager vs a worker:

1. Port: 18818 (as specified)
2. Name: eta-manager (Legal & Compliance Manager)
3. Workspace: /Users/landonking/openclaw-army/agents/eta-manager
4. Session visibility: "all" (managers can see all sessions)
5. Cross-context messaging: enabled (managers need to communicate)
6. Elevated tools: enabled (managers have elevated access)
7. Web search/fetch: enabled (legal research needs)

Since it's a manager, I should follow the alpha-manager pattern more than the general-1 pattern:
- sessions.visibility: "all"
- message.allowCrossContextSend: true
- elevated.enabled: true
- tools.exec: not restricted to "ask: always"

Let me also check if the eta-manager directory exists
Good, directory created. Now let me write the open
